# eia-renewable-energy-analysis
# U.S. Renewable Energy Time Series Analysis
### Solar, Wind & Hydroelectric Power | EIA Data 1973–2025

**BUS 267 | Ambrosia Ardenvale-Wood | March 2026**

---{
  "nbformat": 4,
  "nbformat_minor": 0,
  "metadata": {

  "cells": [
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "2F_bM0mLtSEJ"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "# Renewable Energy Time Series Analysis: Solar, Wind, and Hydroelectric Power in the U.S.\n",
        "\n",
        "## Business Context\n",
        "\n",
        "This analysis is framed for a U.S. energy investment firm seeking to optimize\n",
        "their renewable portfolio. The central business hypothesis is: Can total renewable energy consumption be predicted based on the individual performance\n",
        "of solar, wind, and hydroelectric sources, and use of those predictions inform grid stability planning and investment decisions?\n",
        "\n",
        "## Project Overview\n",
        "This project analyzes U.S. renewable energy consumption patterns from 1973-2025, focusing on three major sources: solar, wind, and hydroelectric power. Using time series decomposition, linear regression modeling, forecasting, and correlation analysis, the analysis examines how these sources have evolved, how they complement each other seasonally, and what future trends may look like through 2027.\n",
        "\n",
        "**Key Questions:**\n",
        "- How have solar, wind, and hydro consumption patterns changed over the past 50 years?\n",
        "- What seasonal patterns exist, and how do these sources complement each other?\n",
        "- What does the future trajectory look like for each renewable source?\n",
        "- Can individual source performance predict total renewable consumption?\n",
        "\n",
        "**Data Source:** U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) Monthly Energy Review, Table 10.1\n",
        "\n",
        "**Data URL:** https://www.eia.gov/totalenergy/data/browser/?tbl=T10.01\n",
        "\n",
        "**Tools & Methods:** Python, pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, statsmodels (seasonal decomposition), scikit-learn (linear regression), Prophet (forecasting)"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "hlIqJryEO0Kb"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 1. Data Loading and Initial Setup\n",
        "This section imports necessary libraries and loads the EIA renewable energy dataset."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "eYo2gX4exFV0"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "execution_count": 1,
      "metadata": {
        "id": "HUA4c0O0P83u"
      },
      "outputs": [],
      "source": [
        "# Import libraries\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
        "import seaborn as sns\n",
        "\n",
        "# Set visualization style\n",
        "plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid')\n",
        "sns.set_palette('husl')\n",
        "\n",
        "# Display settings\n",
        "pd.set_option('display.max_columns', None)\n",
        "pd.set_option('display.max_rows', 100)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Load the EIA renewable energy data\n",
        "df = pd.read_csv('MER_T10_01.csv', na_values=['Not Available'])\n",
        "\n",
        "# Initial exploration\n",
        "print('Dataset shape:', df.shape)\n",
        "print('\\nFirst few rows:')\n",
        "df.head(10)"
      ],

      "source": [
        "# Identify available energy sources in the dataset\n",
        "print('Unique energy sources in dataset:')\n",
        "print(df['Description'].unique())"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {

        },
        "id": "t_sXZzhV1O1X",
        "outputId": "c4e696e7-6ff6-4406-abed-c47cc5c7a81d"
      },
      "execution_count": 3,

          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Unique energy sources in dataset:\n",
            "['Wood Energy Production' 'Biofuels Production'\n",
            " 'Total Biomass Energy Production' 'Total Renewable Energy Production'\n",
            " 'Hydroelectric Power Consumption' 'Geothermal Energy Consumption'\n",
            " 'Solar Energy Consumption' 'Wind Energy Consumption'\n",
            " 'Wood Energy Consumption' 'Waste Energy Consumption'\n",
            " 'Biofuels Consumption' 'Total Biomass Energy Consumption'\n",
            " 'Total Renewable Energy Consumption']\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 2. Data Cleaning and Preparation\n",
        "The EIA dataset contains multiple energy sources including biofuels, wood, and geothermal. For this analysis, the focus is specifically on **solar, wind, and hydroelectric power**, the three major zero-emission renewable sources. Annual totals (month code 13) are filtered out to retain only monthly data, providing the granularity needed for seasonal analysis."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "MAvSrI6Y2lwd"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Filter to solar, wind, and hydroelectric sources only\n",
        "renewable_sources = [\n",
        "    'Solar Energy Consumption',\n",
        "    'Wind Energy Consumption',\n",
        "    'Hydroelectric Power Consumption'\n",
        "]\n",
        "\n",
        "df_clean = df[df['Description'].isin(renewable_sources)].copy()\n",
        "\n",
        "print(f'Filtered dataset shape: {df_clean.shape}')\n",
        "print(f'\\nDate range: {df_clean[\"YYYYMM\"].min()} to {df_clean[\"YYYYMM\"].max()}')\n",
        "print(f'\\nSources included:')\n",
        "print(df_clean['Description'].value_counts())"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "vkNz7RRC2SjP",
        "outputId": "a2861b79-bd29-4ea2-a55d-824dccf9e85e"
      },
      "execution_count": 4,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Filtered dataset shape: (2130, 6)\n",
            "\n",
            "Date range: 194913 to 202510\n",
            "\n",
            "Sources included:\n",
            "Description\n",
            "Hydroelectric Power Consumption    710\n",
            "Solar Energy Consumption           710\n",
            "Wind Energy Consumption            710\n",
            "Name: count, dtype: int64\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Filter out annual totals (month code 13) and parse dates\n",
        "df_clean = df_clean[df_clean['YYYYMM'] % 100 != 13].copy()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Extract year and month, then construct proper datetime column\n",
        "df_clean['Year'] = df_clean['YYYYMM'] // 100\n",
        "df_clean['Month'] = df_clean['YYYYMM'] % 100\n",
        "df_clean['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_clean[['Year', 'Month']].assign(Day=1))\n",
        "\n",
        "# Verify date parsing\n",
        "print(f'After filtering out annual totals: {df_clean.shape}')\n",
        "print(f'\\nDate range: {df_clean[\"Date\"].min()} to {df_clean[\"Date\"].max()}')\n",
        "print(f'\\nFirst few rows with proper dates:')\n",
        "print(df_clean[['Date', 'Description', 'Value']].head(10))"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "UNlpWE374cxl",
        "outputId": "ce5b8dfe-cb61-435c-898e-c79f7b299668"
      },
      "execution_count": 5,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "After filtering out annual totals: (1902, 9)\n",
            "\n",
            "Date range: 1973-01-01 00:00:00 to 2025-10-01 00:00:00\n",
            "\n",
            "First few rows with proper dates:\n",
            "           Date                      Description   Value\n",
            "2864 1973-01-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  89.562\n",
            "2865 1973-02-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  79.544\n",
            "2866 1973-03-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  88.284\n",
            "2867 1973-04-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  83.152\n",
            "2868 1973-05-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  85.643\n",
            "2869 1973-06-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  82.060\n",
            "2870 1973-07-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  77.400\n",
            "2871 1973-08-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  72.936\n",
            "2872 1973-09-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  59.029\n",
            "2873 1973-10-01  Hydroelectric Power Consumption  62.967\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Reshape from long to wide format (one column per energy source)\n",
        "df_wide = df_clean.pivot(index='Date', columns='Description', values='Value')\n",
        "\n",
        "# Rename columns for readability\n",
        "df_wide.columns = ['Hydro', 'Solar', 'Wind']\n",
        "\n",
        "# Verify structure and check for missing values\n",
        "print(df_wide.head(10))\n",
        "print(f'\\nDataset shape: {df_wide.shape}')\n",
        "print(f'\\nMissing values:\\n{df_wide.isnull().sum()}')"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "HkDbjWey5uFE",
        "outputId": "4b674480-9c7b-4841-c475-f602b8c5386f"
      },
      "execution_count": 6,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "             Hydro  Solar  Wind\n",
            "Date                           \n",
            "1973-01-01  89.562    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-02-01  79.544    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-03-01  88.284    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-04-01  83.152    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-05-01  85.643    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-06-01  82.060    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-07-01  77.400    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-08-01  72.936    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-09-01  59.029    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-10-01  62.967    NaN   NaN\n",
            "\n",
            "Dataset shape: (634, 3)\n",
            "\n",
            "Missing values:\n",
            "Hydro      0\n",
            "Solar    132\n",
            "Wind     120\n",
            "dtype: int64\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Verify data types and confirm start dates for each source\n",
        "print(df_wide.head(10))\n",
        "print(f'\\nMissing values:\\n{df_wide.isnull().sum()}')\n",
        "print(f'\\nFirst available data point by source:')\n",
        "print(f'Hydro: {df_wide[\"Hydro\"].first_valid_index()}')\n",
        "print(f'Solar: {df_wide[\"Solar\"].first_valid_index()}')\n",
        "print(f'Wind: {df_wide[\"Wind\"].first_valid_index()}')"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "bFJySSzC6qYC",
        "outputId": "225948e6-ab07-47d0-f0b1-761d256b3ba2"
      },
      "execution_count": 7,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "             Hydro  Solar  Wind\n",
            "Date                           \n",
            "1973-01-01  89.562    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-02-01  79.544    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-03-01  88.284    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-04-01  83.152    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-05-01  85.643    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-06-01  82.060    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-07-01  77.400    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-08-01  72.936    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-09-01  59.029    NaN   NaN\n",
            "1973-10-01  62.967    NaN   NaN\n",
            "\n",
            "Missing values:\n",
            "Hydro      0\n",
            "Solar    132\n",
            "Wind     120\n",
            "dtype: int64\n",
            "\n",
            "First available data point by source:\n",
            "Hydro: 1973-01-01 00:00:00\n",
            "Solar: 1984-01-01 00:00:00\n",
            "Wind: 1983-01-01 00:00:00\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Fill early-year NaN values with 0 for Solar and Wind\n",
        "# These missing values represent periods before utility-scale reporting began,\n",
        "# not true missing data — zero is the accurate value for those periods\n",
        "df_wide['Solar'] = df_wide['Solar'].fillna(0)\n",
        "df_wide['Wind'] = df_wide['Wind'].fillna(0)\n",
        "\n",
        "print('Missing values after zero-fill:')\n",
        "print(df_wide.isnull().sum())\n",
        "print('\\nNote: Solar and Wind zeros represent pre-utility-scale reporting periods')\n",
        "print(f'Solar zeros: {(df_wide[\"Solar\"] == 0).sum()} months (pre-1984)')\n",
        "print(f'Wind zeros: {(df_wide[\"Wind\"] == 0).sum()} months (pre-1983)')"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "PxC9KqD08Pza",
        "outputId": "df4d8ee3-cee4-4643-c012-8b1145b1ab2d"
      },
      "execution_count": 8,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Missing values after zero-fill:\n",
            "Hydro    0\n",
            "Solar    0\n",
            "Wind     0\n",
            "dtype: int64\n",
            "\n",
            "Note: Solar and Wind zeros represent pre-utility-scale reporting periods\n",
            "Solar zeros: 141 months (pre-1984)\n",
            "Wind zeros: 148 months (pre-1983)\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### Duplicate Checking\n",
        "As part of standard data cleaning, the dataset was checked for duplicate records."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "2mEi9fIGWbX7"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Check for and remove duplicate records\n",
        "duplicates = df_wide.duplicated().sum()\n",
        "print(f'Duplicate rows found: {duplicates}')\n",
        "df_wide = df_wide.drop_duplicates()\n",
        "print(f'Dataset shape after duplicate check: {df_wide.shape}')"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "Fu6LQs0WWPTL",
        "outputId": "efaa400d-57a6-4c05-d369-a439b72a558c"
      },
      "execution_count": 9,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Duplicate rows found: 0\n",
            "Dataset shape after duplicate check: (634, 3)\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Feature Engineering: Adding time-based features to capture seasonality\n",
        "# Season as a categorical variable for business interpretability\n",
        "\n",
        "df_wide['Month'] = df_wide.index.month\n",
        "df_wide['Year'] = df_wide.index.year\n",
        "\n",
        "def get_season(month):\n",
        "  if month in [12, 1, 2]:\n",
        "      return 'Winter'\n",
        "  elif month in [3, 4, 5]:\n",
        "      return 'Spring'\n",
        "  elif month in [6, 7, 8]:\n",
        "      return 'Summer'\n",
        "  else:\n",
        "      return 'Fall'\n",
        "\n",
        "df_wide['Season'] = df_wide['Month'].apply(get_season)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Create Total_Renewables as regression target variable\n",
        "df_wide['Total_Renewables'] = df_wide['Hydro'] + df_wide['Solar'] + df_wide['Wind']\n",
        "\n",
        "print('Feature engineering complete. Updated dataframe:')\n",
        "print(df_wide.head(12))\n",
        "print(f'\\nSeason distribution:')\n",
        "print(df_wide['Season'].value_counts())"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "dz9kc23rXYfs",
        "outputId": "9200600b-500e-45df-dc7a-87d32a562920"
      },
      "execution_count": 10,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Feature engineering complete. Updated dataframe:\n",
            "             Hydro  Solar  Wind  Month  Year  Season  Total_Renewables\n",
            "Date                                                                  \n",
            "1973-01-01  89.562    0.0   0.0      1  1973  Winter            89.562\n",
            "1973-02-01  79.544    0.0   0.0      2  1973  Winter            79.544\n",
            "1973-03-01  88.284    0.0   0.0      3  1973  Spring            88.284\n",
            "1973-04-01  83.152    0.0   0.0      4  1973  Spring            83.152\n",
            "1973-05-01  85.643    0.0   0.0      5  1973  Spring            85.643\n",
            "1973-06-01  82.060    0.0   0.0      6  1973  Summer            82.060\n",
            "1973-07-01  77.400    0.0   0.0      7  1973  Summer            77.400\n",
            "1973-08-01  72.936    0.0   0.0      8  1973  Summer            72.936\n",
            "1973-09-01  59.029    0.0   0.0      9  1973    Fall            59.029\n",
            "1973-10-01  62.967    0.0   0.0     10  1973    Fall            62.967\n",
            "1973-11-01  69.063    0.0   0.0     11  1973    Fall            69.063\n",
            "1973-12-01  90.131    0.0   0.0     12  1973  Winter            90.131\n",
            "\n",
            "Season distribution:\n",
            "Season\n",
            "Spring    159\n",
            "Summer    159\n",
            "Winter    158\n",
            "Fall      158\n",
            "Name: count, dtype: int64\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Export cleaned and feature-engineered dataset for submission\n",
        "# df_wide.to_csv('EIA_energy_cleaned.csv')\n",
        "# Run once to generate file, then comment out"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "dMMeofR9gJls"
      },
      "execution_count": 11,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 3. Exploratory Data Analysis\n",
        "\n",
        "### 3.1 Summary Statistics\n",
        "Before visualizing trends, summary statistics provide a quantitative foundation for understanding the distribution and volatility of each energy source."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "kEsV6Ts-kkD4"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Summary statistics for all three renewable sources\n",
        "summary = df_wide[['Hydro', 'Solar', 'Wind', 'Total_Renewables']].describe()\n",
        "print(summary.round(2))"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "F0yqATtvj_9y",
        "outputId": "4a1c9704-940b-4055-e9ea-40c2c046bcb7"
      },
      "execution_count": 12,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "        Hydro   Solar    Wind  Total_Renewables\n",
            "count  634.00  634.00  634.00            634.00\n",
            "mean    79.32   13.32   24.95            117.59\n",
            "std     14.14   24.74   40.27             61.93\n",
            "min     49.02    0.00    0.00             52.26\n",
            "25%     68.84    0.00    0.00             78.18\n",
            "50%     78.41    4.98    1.52             93.45\n",
            "75%     88.86    8.06   40.08            125.59\n",
            "max    119.40  153.26  172.67            361.12\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### Interpretation\n",
        "These summary statistics reveal distinct characteristics across renewable sources that carry direct implications for energy investment planning:\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Hydroelectric**: Highest and most stable mean consumption (79.32 Trillion Btu), but a standard deviation of 14.14 signals meaningful climate-driven variability. For portfolio planning purposes, hydro is reliable but not immune to drought risk.\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Solar**: A mean of 13.32 Trillion Btu masks explosive recent growth: the gap between the 50th percentile (4.98) and maximum (153.26) communicates that solar is a young, dynamic asset class.\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Wind**: Similar to solar, with a median of just 1.52 Trillion Btu but a maximum of 172.67, the highest of any single source. Wind has already surpassed hydro at peak output.\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Total Renewables**: A standard deviation of 61.93 against a mean of 117.59 reflects the portfolio-level volatility an energy firm must plan around. Diversification across all three sources is essential for grid stability."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "q5O-LIk-l23r"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### 3.2 Overall Trends (1973-2025)\n",
        "\n",
        "This visualization shows the complete history of renewable energy consumption across all three sources. Hydroelectric power has been relatively stable for decades, while wind and solar have experienced exponential growth starting in the 2000s and 2010s respectively."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "ux8_bnusoUz7"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# U.S. renewable energy consumption trends (1973-2025)\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.plot(df_wide.index, df_wide['Hydro'], label='Hydro', linewidth=2.5,\n",
        "         color='#fc8d82', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.plot(df_wide.index, df_wide['Solar'], label='Solar', linewidth=2.5,\n",
        "         color='#e5ae38', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.plot(df_wide.index, df_wide['Wind'], label='Wind', linewidth=2.5,\n",
        "         color='#83d0c9', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.title('U.S. Renewable Energy Consumption: Solar, Wind, and Hydro (1973-2025)',\n",
        "          fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', pad=20)\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Year', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Energy Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.legend(fontsize=11, loc='upper left')\n",
        "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 536
        },
        "id": "Ym0BDBr0pgvG",
        "outputId": "9e7012a0-7e17-42c6-c1a9-8dcd3ed8a5ef"
      },
      "execution_count": 13,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x700 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### 3.3 Recent Growth Patterns (2010-2025)\n",
        "\n",
        "Zooming in on the past 15 years reveals the renewable energy boom more clearly. Wind energy has surpassed hydroelectric power as the dominant renewable source, while solar shows the steepest growth trajectory despite starting from a much smaller base."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "c6fzHiPbscgY"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Renewable energy consumption trends, zoomed to recent growth period (2010-2025)\n",
        "df_recent = df_wide['2010':]\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.plot(df_recent.index, df_recent['Hydro'], label='Hydro', linewidth=2.5,\n",
        "         color='#fc8d82', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.plot(df_recent.index, df_recent['Solar'], label='Solar', linewidth=2.5,\n",
        "         color='#e5ae38', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.plot(df_recent.index, df_recent['Wind'], label='Wind', linewidth=2.5,\n",
        "         color='#83d0c9', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.title('Recent Renewable Energy Growth (2010-2025)',\n",
        "          fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', pad=20)\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Year', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Energy Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.legend(fontsize=11, loc='upper left')\n",
        "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 536
        },
        "id": "GNbOwsh2tNlO",
        "outputId": "8cb5ead8-bb84-4f29-eec9-5923ab74be41"
      },
      "execution_count": 14,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x700 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### 3.4 Seasonal Correlation\n",
        "\n",
        "Averaging consumption by month reveals distinct seasonal patterns for each source:\n",
        "\n",
        "- **Hydro** peaks in spring (April-May) during snowmelt season\n",
        "- **Solar** peaks in summer (June-July) with maximum sun exposure\n",
        "- **Wind** peaks in winter/spring (December-March) with stronger seasonal winds\n",
        "\n",
        "These complementary patterns suggest natural load balancing across the year."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "vc-TaXwexwH6"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Average monthly consumption by source (2015-2025) to reveal seasonal patterns\n",
        "df_seasonal = df_wide['2015':].copy()\n",
        "df_seasonal['Month'] = df_seasonal.index.month\n",
        "monthly_avg = df_seasonal.groupby('Month')[['Hydro', 'Solar', 'Wind']].mean()\n",
        "\n",
        "month_names = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May', 'Jun',\n",
        "               'Jul', 'Aug', 'Sep', 'Oct', 'Nov', 'Dec']\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.plot(monthly_avg.index, monthly_avg['Hydro'], marker='o', label='Hydro',\n",
        "         linewidth=2.5, markersize=8, color='#fc8d82', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.plot(monthly_avg.index, monthly_avg['Solar'], marker='o', label='Solar',\n",
        "         linewidth=2.5, markersize=8, color='#e5ae38', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.plot(monthly_avg.index, monthly_avg['Wind'], marker='o', label='Wind',\n",
        "         linewidth=2.5, markersize=8, color='#83d0c9', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.title('Average Monthly Renewable Energy Consumption (2015-2025)',\n",
        "          fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', pad=20)\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Month', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.ylabel('Average Energy Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.xticks(range(1, 13), month_names)\n",
        "plt.legend(fontsize=11)\n",
        "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()\n"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 536
        },
        "id": "tOd3AtNiz2-7",
        "outputId": "4cf8bb1f-c6b6-4917-876d-670e786a7b25"
      },
      "execution_count": 15,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1200x600 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### 3.5 Scatter Plots: Individual Sources vs Total Renewable Consumption\n",
        "\n",
        "These scatter plots visualize the relationship between each individual energy source and total renewable consumption, a key step in understanding which sources drive overall portfolio performance."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "EzbCUhYV3jYG"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Scatter plots: Individual sources vs Total Renewable Consumption\n",
        "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))\n",
        "\n",
        "# Solar vs Total Renewables\n",
        "axes[0].scatter(df_wide['Solar'], df_wide['Total_Renewables'],\n",
        "                color='#e5ae38', alpha=0.5, s=20)\n",
        "axes[0].set_xlabel('Solar Energy Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "axes[0].set_ylabel('Total Renewable Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "axes[0].set_title('Solar vs Total Renewable Consumption',\n",
        "                  fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Wind vs Total Renewables\n",
        "axes[1].scatter(df_wide['Wind'], df_wide['Total_Renewables'],\n",
        "                color='#83d0c9', alpha=0.5, s=20)\n",
        "axes[1].set_xlabel('Wind Energy Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "axes[1].set_ylabel('Total Renewable Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "axes[1].set_title('Wind vs Total Renewable Consumption',\n",
        "                  fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 462
        },
        "id": "s09Bv4ct4DfC",
        "outputId": "c43245c5-76db-417b-ac99-72d4486d2603"
      },
      "execution_count": 16,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x600 with 2 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### Interpretation\n",
        "Both scatter plots reveal strong positive relationships between individual renewable sources and total consumption. As Solar and Wind output increases, total renewable consumption rises correspondingly, confirming that these sources are primary drivers of portfolio growth rather than substitutes for each other.\n",
        "\n",
        "The dense cluster of points near zero on both plots reflects the early reporting period before utility-scale Solar and Wind capacity existed (pre-1984 for Solar, pre-1983 for Wind), where zero values were appropriately assigned during data cleaning.\n",
        "\n",
        "These relationships support the regression hypothesis: Solar and Wind are meaningful predictors of Total Renewable Consumption."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "iBsKD5nH67IQ"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### 3.6 Correlation Heatmap\n",
        "\n",
        "This heatmap quantifies the relationships between individual renewable sources\n",
        "and total consumption, providing a statistical foundation for the regression\n",
        "model that follows."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "77NtCzSS9n0h"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Correlation heatmap: renewable sources and total consumption (2015-2025)\n",
        "from matplotlib.colors import LinearSegmentedColormap\n",
        "\n",
        "df_corr_eda = df_wide['2015':][['Hydro', 'Solar', 'Wind',\n",
        "                                 'Total_Renewables']].copy()\n",
        "\n",
        "custom_cmap = LinearSegmentedColormap.from_list(\n",
        "    'custom', ['#83d0c9', 'white', '#fc8d82'], N=256)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))\n",
        "sns.heatmap(df_corr_eda.corr(), annot=True, fmt='.3f',\n",
        "            cmap=custom_cmap,\n",
        "            center=0, vmin=-1, vmax=1, square=True, linewidths=2,\n",
        "            linecolor='white', alpha=0.8,\n",
        "            annot_kws={'size': 12, 'weight': 'bold'})\n",
        "plt.title('Renewable Energy Source Correlation Including Total Consumption (2015-2025)',\n",
        "          fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', pad=15)\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 607
        },
        "id": "VvfxG0PG9Vk0",
        "outputId": "fe6f5faf-1322-454a-d950-c7968e2eabe9"
      },
      "execution_count": 17,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 800x600 with 2 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### Interpretation\n",
        "Solar and Wind show strong positive correlations with Total Renewable Consumption (0.877 and 0.866 respectively), confirming they are the primary drivers of overall portfolio growth. Hydroelectric power shows near-zero correlation (0.037) with total consumption, consistent with its role as a stable but non-growing baseline resource. The moderate positive correlation between Solar and Wind (0.599) reflects their shared growth trajectory over the past decade rather than a physical relationship between the two sources."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "j9DhbLCD__3f"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# ADF Stationarity Test\n",
        "# Formally tests whether each time series has a unit root (non-stationary)\n",
        "# H0: The series has a unit root (non-stationary)\n",
        "# H1: The series is stationary\n",
        "from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller\n",
        "\n",
        "for source in ['Hydro', 'Solar', 'Wind']:\n",
        "    series = df_wide[source].dropna()\n",
        "    result = adfuller(series)\n",
        "    print(f'{source}:')\n",
        "    print(f'  ADF Statistic: {result[0]:.4f}')\n",
        "    print(f'  p-value: {result[1]:.4f}')\n",
        "    print(f'  Stationary: {\"No\" if result[1] > 0.05 else \"Yes\"}')\n",
        "    print()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "KJbasZTpA9tH",
        "outputId": "c8d62b0d-aada-4dc7-b324-d06af350a3b2"
      },
      "execution_count": 18,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Hydro:\n",
            "  ADF Statistic: -4.6704\n",
            "  p-value: 0.0001\n",
            "  Stationary: Yes\n",
            "\n",
            "Solar:\n",
            "  ADF Statistic: 5.4569\n",
            "  p-value: 1.0000\n",
            "  Stationary: No\n",
            "\n",
            "Wind:\n",
            "  ADF Statistic: 1.8848\n",
            "  p-value: 0.9985\n",
            "  Stationary: No\n",
            "\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Solar and Wind are non-stationary (p > 0.05), confirming strong growth trends that justify multiplicative decomposition. Hydro is stationary (p < 0.05), consistent with its stable long-term baseline and additive decomposition approach."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "sIIiTXV2CoTr"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 4. Time Series Decomposition\n",
        "\n",
        "Time series decomposition separates each energy source into three components:\n",
        "- **Trend:** Long-term growth or decline\n",
        "- **Seasonal:** Repeating annual patterns\n",
        "- **Residual:** Random noise and one-off events\n",
        "\n",
        "### 4.1 Solar Energy Decomposition\n",
        "\n",
        "Solar shows a clear exponential growth trend with highly regular seasonal cycles. The residuals are relatively small, indicating predictable behavior."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "-RodfBgMBy33"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import seasonal_decompose\n",
        "\n",
        "# Time series decomposition using 2015-2024 (10 complete years)\n",
        "df_decomp = df_wide['2015':'2024'].copy()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Decompose each source (additive for stable hydro, multiplicative for exponential solar/wind)\n",
        "decomp_hydro = seasonal_decompose(df_decomp['Hydro'].dropna(), model='additive', period=12)\n",
        "decomp_solar = seasonal_decompose(df_decomp['Solar'].dropna(), model='multiplicative', period=12)\n",
        "decomp_wind = seasonal_decompose(df_decomp['Wind'].dropna(), model='multiplicative', period=12)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Visualize solar decomposition: observed, trend, seasonal, and residual components\n",
        "fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 1, figsize=(14, 10))\n",
        "\n",
        "decomp_solar.observed.plot(ax=axes[0], title='Solar Energy: Time Series Decomposition',\n",
        "                           color='#7a9cc6', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[0].set_ylabel('Observed')\n",
        "axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "decomp_solar.trend.plot(ax=axes[1], color='#fc8d82', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[1].set_ylabel('Trend')\n",
        "axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "decomp_solar.seasonal.plot(ax=axes[2], color='#e5ae38', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[2].set_ylabel('Seasonal')\n",
        "axes[2].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "decomp_solar.resid.plot(ax=axes[3], color='#83d0c9', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[3].set_ylabel('Residual')\n",
        "axes[3].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 762
        },
        "id": "J7cPYd_wFBSb",
        "outputId": "c07e34f3-dddb-46d9-af0e-ebe5a302d018"
      },
      "execution_count": 19,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x1000 with 4 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### 4.2 Comparing Trends Across Sources\n",
        "\n",
        "Extracting just the trend components reveals fundamentally different trajectories:\n",
        "- **Hydro** is declining, likely due to drought and water management constraints\n",
        "- **Solar** shows steady exponential growth\n",
        "- **Wind** grew rapidly through 2022 but has plateaued recently"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "aOTRmGYNHfEn"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Compare extracted trend components across all three sources\n",
        "fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(14, 10), sharex=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Hydro trend\n",
        "decomp_hydro.trend.plot(ax=axes[0], color='#fc8d82', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[0].set_title('Hydro: Long-term Trend (2015-2024)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[0].set_ylabel('Trillion Btu', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Solar trend\n",
        "decomp_solar.trend.plot(ax=axes[1], color='#e5ae38', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[1].set_title('Solar: Long-term Trend (2015-2024)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[1].set_ylabel('Trillion Btu', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Wind trend\n",
        "decomp_wind.trend.plot(ax=axes[2], color='#83d0c9', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[2].set_title('Wind: Long-term Trend (2015-2024)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[2].set_ylabel('Trillion Btu', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[2].set_xlabel('Year', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[2].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 762
        },
        "id": "NqY4Wz-_Htm9",
        "outputId": "8167bd2e-4f0b-4d71-e80f-ceed120891c7"
      },
      "execution_count": 20,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x1000 with 3 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Rolling Year-over-Year Growth Rate: Wind Energy\n",
        "# Validates the \"plateauing\" observation by quantifying declining growth rate\n",
        "wind_series = df_wide['Wind'].dropna()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Calculate YoY growth rate (comparing each month to same month prior year)\n",
        "wind_yoy = wind_series.pct_change(periods=12) * 100\n",
        "\n",
        "# Calculate 12-month rolling average to smooth monthly volatility\n",
        "wind_yoy_rolling = wind_yoy.rolling(window=12).mean()\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(14, 6))\n",
        "plt.plot(wind_yoy_rolling.index, wind_yoy_rolling,\n",
        "         color='#83d0c9', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.axhline(y=0, color='#95A5A6', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.7)\n",
        "plt.title('Wind Energy: Rolling Year-over-Year Growth Rate',\n",
        "          fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', pad=20)\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Year', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.ylabel('YoY Growth Rate (%)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 461
        },
        "id": "EjrV8kVBKDhy",
        "outputId": "7104d22b-1f3c-45c5-8374-20497cc76a5e"
      },
      "execution_count": 21,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x600 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Zoom in to 2005-2025 to better visualize the plateauing growth rate\n",
        "plt.figure(figsize=(14, 6))\n",
        "plt.plot(wind_yoy_rolling['2005':].index, wind_yoy_rolling['2005':],\n",
        "         color='#83d0c9', linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "plt.axhline(y=0, color='#95A5A6', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.7)\n",
        "plt.title('Wind Energy: Rolling YoY Growth Rate (2005-2025)',\n",
        "          fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', pad=20)\n",
        "plt.xlabel('Year', fontsize=12)\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 461
        },
        "id": "2A_n0KGRLYVl",
        "outputId": "577ba4ba-0eb3-4758-901f-78c39fd84814"
      },
      "execution_count": 22,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x600 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Linear Regression: Modeling Long-term Trends in Renewable Energy Consumption\n",
        "# Dependent variable: Energy consumption (Trillion Btu)\n",
        "# Independent variable: Time (years elapsed since first observation)\n",
        "from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression\n",
        "from sklearn.metrics import r2_score, mean_absolute_error\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "\n",
        "fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(14, 12))\n",
        "\n",
        "results = {}\n",
        "\n",
        "for idx, source in enumerate(['Solar', 'Wind', 'Hydro']):\n",
        "    series = df_wide[source].dropna()\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Convert dates to numeric (years elapsed)\n",
        "    X = np.array((series.index - series.index[0]).days / 365.25).reshape(-1, 1)\n",
        "    y = series.values\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Fit linear regression\n",
        "    model = LinearRegression()\n",
        "    model.fit(X, y)\n",
        "    y_pred = model.predict(X)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Store results\n",
        "    results[source] = {\n",
        "        'slope': model.coef_[0],\n",
        "        'intercept': model.intercept_,\n",
        "        'r2': r2_score(y, y_pred),\n",
        "        'mae': mean_absolute_error(y, y_pred)\n",
        "    }\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Plot\n",
        "    colors = {'Solar': '#e5ae38', 'Wind': '#83d0c9', 'Hydro': '#fc8d82'}\n",
        "    axes[idx].scatter(series.index, y, color=colors[source],\n",
        "                      alpha=0.4, s=10, label='Actual')\n",
        "\n",
        "    axes[idx].plot(series.index, y_pred, color='#7a9cc6',\n",
        "                   linewidth=2.5, label='Linear Trend')\n",
        "    axes[idx].set_title(f'{source} Energy: Linear Regression Trend',\n",
        "                        fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "    axes[idx].set_ylabel('Trillion Btu', fontsize=11)\n",
        "    axes[idx].legend(fontsize=10)\n",
        "    axes[idx].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Print regression results\n",
        "print('\\nLinear Regression Results:')\n",
        "print('='*36)\n",
        "for source, res in results.items():\n",
        "    print(f'\\n{source}:')\n",
        "    print(f'  Slope (Trillion Btu/year): {res[\"slope\"]:.4f}')\n",
        "    print(f'  Intercept: {res[\"intercept\"]:.4f}')\n",
        "    print(f'  R-squared: {res[\"r2\"]:.4f}')\n",
        "    print(f'  MAE: {res[\"mae\"]:.4f}')"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 1000
        },
        "id": "vuDBuqXGN__m",
        "outputId": "0e2b5b19-cdc3-4c91-a973-3b2c1f93ca05"
      },
      "execution_count": 23,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x1200 with 3 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        },
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "\n",
            "Linear Regression Results:\n",
            "====================================\n",
            "\n",
            "Solar:\n",
            "  Slope (Trillion Btu/year): 1.0791\n",
            "  Intercept: -15.1433\n",
            "  R-squared: 0.4434\n",
            "  MAE: 12.1147\n",
            "\n",
            "Wind:\n",
            "  Slope (Trillion Btu/year): 2.0986\n",
            "  Intercept: -30.3940\n",
            "  R-squared: 0.6328\n",
            "  MAE: 19.7899\n",
            "\n",
            "Hydro:\n",
            "  Slope (Trillion Btu/year): -0.1498\n",
            "  Intercept: 83.2720\n",
            "  R-squared: 0.0262\n",
            "  MAE: 11.4205\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "Linear regression confirms three distinct trajectories: Wind shows the strongest linear trend (+2.93 Trillion Btu/year, R²=0.72), Solar's low R² (0.49) reflects exponential rather than linear growth, and Hydro's near-zero R² (0.03) confirms no meaningful long-term trend, consistent with seasonal oscillation around a stable baseline."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "bP7A7NOzTrCF"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## Business Implications of Linear Regression Findings\n",
        "\n",
        "**Wind Energy** is the most predictable renewable investment in the near term,\n",
        "with a consistent linear growth rate of +2.93 Trillion Btu/year and the strongest model fit (R²=0.72). Energy planners can forecast wind capacity additions with\n",
        "reasonable confidence using historical trend data alone.\n",
        "\n",
        "**Solar Energy** is growing faster than any linear model can capture. The\n",
        "exponential trajectory suggests that linear regression systematically underestimates future solar output, meaning solar represents the most growth potential in the renewable portfolio, but requires nonlinear modeling\n",
        "(such as Prophet) for accurate forecasting.\n",
        "\n",
        "**Hydroelectric Power** shows no statistically meaningful growth trend\n",
        "(R²=0.03, slope=-0.15). For portfolio planning purposes, hydro should be treated as a stable but plateaued baseline resource, subject primarily to climate variability rather than capacity expansion.\n",
        "\n",
        "**Portfolio Recommendation:** A diversified renewable strategy should prioritize solar for long-term growth, rely on wind for predictable near-future capacity additions, and treat hydro as a stable seasonal complement rather than a growth asset."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "kQhrM7qjU0Bd"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Install Prophet if not already available\n",
        "# Uncomment the line below if Prophet is not installed:\n",
        "# !pip install prophet"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "PucWlThhWRSL"
      },
      "execution_count": 24,
      "outputs": []
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "## 5. Forecasting Future Consumption (2026-2027)\n",
        "\n",
        "Using Facebook's Prophet model, renewable energy consumption is forecasted 24 months into the future. Prophet is well-suited for this task because it automatically handles:\n",
        "- Seasonal patterns (yearly cycles)\n",
        "- Growth trends (linear or exponential)\n",
        "- Uncertainty intervals (showing prediction confidence)\n",
        "\n",
        "The forecasts assume historical patterns continue without major policy changes or technological disruptions."
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "538rtOVMWe2m"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "from prophet import Prophet\n",
        "import warnings\n",
        "warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')\n",
        "\n",
        "# Prepare solar data for Prophet forecasting (requires 'ds' and 'y' column names)\n",
        "solar_prophet = df_wide[['Solar']].reset_index()\n",
        "solar_prophet.columns = ['ds', 'y']\n",
        "solar_prophet = solar_prophet.dropna()\n",
        "\n",
        "# Initialize and fit Prophet model with multiplicative seasonality for exponential growth\n",
        "model_solar = Prophet(\n",
        "    yearly_seasonality=True,\n",
        "    weekly_seasonality=False,\n",
        "    daily_seasonality=False,\n",
        "    seasonality_mode='multiplicative'\n",
        ")\n",
        "model_solar.fit(solar_prophet)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Generate 24-month forecast (through 2027)\n",
        "future = model_solar.make_future_dataframe(periods=24, freq='MS')\n",
        "forecast_solar = model_solar.predict(future)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Visualize historical data and forecast with uncertainty intervals\n",
        "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))\n",
        "\n",
        "ax.plot(solar_prophet['ds'], solar_prophet['y'], 'o', color='#e5ae38',\n",
        "        markersize=4, label='Actual', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "ax.plot(forecast_solar['ds'], forecast_solar['yhat'], color='#7a9cc6',\n",
        "        linewidth=2.5, label='Forecast', alpha=0.8)\n",
        "ax.fill_between(forecast_solar['ds'], forecast_solar['yhat_lower'],\n",
        "                forecast_solar['yhat_upper'], color='#7a9cc6', alpha=0.15)\n",
        "\n",
        "ax.set_title('Solar Energy Forecast: 2026-2027', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold', pad=20)\n",
        "ax.set_xlabel('Year', fontsize=12)\n",
        "ax.set_ylabel('Energy Consumption (Trillion Btu)', fontsize=12)\n",
        "ax.legend(fontsize=11)\n",
        "ax.grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/",
          "height": 461
        },
        "id": "tndlTGdrXV1-",
        "outputId": "2f4c1ab4-1cf4-42c4-b549-916c9b28e235"
      },
      "execution_count": 25,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "display_data",
          "data": {
            "text/plain": [
              "<Figure size 1400x600 with 1 Axes>"
            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
          },
          "metadata": {}
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Generate forecasts for wind and hydro using the same 24-month horizon\n",
        "\n",
        "# Wind forecast (multiplicative seasonality for growth trend)\n",
        "wind_prophet = df_wide[['Wind']].reset_index()\n",
        "wind_prophet.columns = ['ds', 'y']\n",
        "wind_prophet = wind_prophet.dropna()\n",
        "\n",
        "model_wind = Prophet(yearly_seasonality=True, seasonality_mode='multiplicative')\n",
        "model_wind.fit(wind_prophet)\n",
        "future_wind = model_wind.make_future_dataframe(periods=24, freq='MS')\n",
        "forecast_wind = model_wind.predict(future_wind)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Hydro forecast (additive seasonality for stable baseline)\n",
        "hydro_prophet = df_wide[['Hydro']].reset_index()\n",
        "hydro_prophet.columns = ['ds', 'y']\n",
        "hydro_prophet = hydro_prophet.dropna()\n",
        "\n",
        "model_hydro = Prophet(yearly_seasonality=True, seasonality_mode='additive')\n",
        "model_hydro.fit(hydro_prophet)\n",
        "future_hydro = model_hydro.make_future_dataframe(periods=24, freq='MS')\n",
        "forecast_hydro = model_hydro.predict(future_hydro)\n",
        "\n",
        "print('All three forecasts complete')"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "iXPcHRJ0b1N7",
        "outputId": "fee9c958-7d3d-4f74-bcbd-379dce9f4148"
      },
      "execution_count": 26,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stderr",
          "text": [
            "INFO:prophet:Disabling weekly seasonality. Run prophet with weekly_seasonality=True to override this.\n",
            "INFO:prophet:Disabling daily seasonality. Run prophet with daily_seasonality=True to override this.\n",
            "INFO:prophet:Disabling weekly seasonality. Run prophet with weekly_seasonality=True to override this.\n",
            "INFO:prophet:Disabling daily seasonality. Run prophet with daily_seasonality=True to override this.\n"
          ]
        },
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "All three forecasts complete\n"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "source": [
        "### 5.1 Forecast Results by Source\n",
        "\n",
        "The models predict:\n",
        "- **Solar** could reach 150+ trillion Btu by late 2027 (nearly doubling current levels)\n",
        "- **Wind** continues steady growth to 175-200 trillion Btu\n",
        "- **Hydro** remains volatile with high seasonal swings but no clear growth trend"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "id": "WeNeePasePa2"
      }
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "source": [
        "# Compare all three forecasts side-by-side with uncertainty intervals\n",
        "fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(14, 12), sharex=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Solar forecast\n",
        "axes[0].plot(solar_prophet['ds'], solar_prophet['y'], 'o', color='#e5ae38',\n",
        "            markersize=4, alpha=0.8, zorder=3)\n",
        "axes[0].plot(forecast_solar['ds'], forecast_solar['yhat'], color='#7a9cc6',\n",
        "            linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[0].fill_between(forecast_solar['ds'], forecast_solar['yhat_lower'],\n",
        "                     forecast_solar['yhat_upper'], color='#7a9cc6', alpha=0.15)\n",
        "axes[0].set_title('Solar Energy Forecast (2026-2027)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[0].set_ylabel('Trillion Btu', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Wind forecast\n",
        "axes[1].plot(wind_prophet['ds'], wind_prophet['y'], 'o', color='#83d0c9',\n",
        "            markersize=4, alpha=0.8, zorder=3)\n",
        "axes[1].plot(forecast_wind['ds'], forecast_wind['yhat'], color='#7a9cc6',\n",
        "            linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[1].fill_between(forecast_wind['ds'], forecast_wind['yhat_lower'],\n",
        "                     forecast_wind['yhat_upper'], color='#7a9cc6', alpha=0.15)\n",
        "axes[1].set_title('Wind Energy Forecast (2026-2027)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[1].set_ylabel('Trillion Btu', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Hydro forecast\n",
        "axes[2].plot(hydro_prophet['ds'], hydro_prophet['y'], 'o', color='#fc8d82',\n",
        "            markersize=4, alpha=0.8, zorder=3)\n",
        "axes[2].plot(forecast_hydro['ds'], forecast_hydro['yhat'], color='#7a9cc6',\n",
        "            linewidth=2.5, alpha=0.8)\n",
        "axes[2].fill_between(forecast_hydro['ds'], forecast_hydro['yhat_lower'],\n",
        "                     forecast_hydro['yhat_upper'], color='#7a9cc6', alpha=0.15)\n",
        "axes[2].set_title('Hydro Energy Forecast (2026-2027)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
        "axes[2].set_ylabel('Trillion Btu', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[2].set_xlabel('Year', fontsize=11)\n",
        "axes[2].grid(True, alpha=0.2)\n",
        "\n",
        "plt.tight_layout()\n",
        "plt.show()"
      ],
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {

            ],
            "image/png": "\n"
